title: Implement TUI Operator Action Handlers
description: Add TUI runtime and keybinding support for one-shot scheduler runs, continuous run-mode toggling, blocked-bead retry, merge invocation through the existing confirmation flow, and confirmed single-bead status updates. Wire actions through existing CLI/scheduler/storage paths, enforce scope-aware behavior for `--feature-root`, reject invalid actions without mutation, and record last action/result/timestamp in the bottom status panel without crashing the TUI on failures.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing `s` runs one scheduler cycle scoped to the current feature root when present and returns control to the TUI after completion.
Pressing `S` toggles continuous run mode for the active scope and the active state is visible in the footer or status panel.
Pressing `t` retries only a selected blocked bead and shows an immediate visible state/result update.
Pressing `m` keeps the existing merge confirmation behavior for selected done beads and reports success or failure in the status panel.
Pressing `u` opens a quick status action flow that supports ready, blocked, and done updates with `y/n` confirmation and no mutation for disallowed transitions.
All operator actions surface clear non-fatal success or failure messages with timestamps in the status panel.
Invalid actions for the selected bead state show a clear message and leave bead state unchanged.
Implementation respects global vs `--feature-root` scope when invoking scheduler and bead mutations.

handoff_summary.completed: Added scope-aware TUI runtime actions in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` for `s`, `S`, `t`, `m`, and `u` flows; tracked last action/result/timestamp in the status panel; preserved non-fatal failure handling; and wired confirmed status updates through new CLI validation helpers in `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py`. Expanded regression coverage in `tests/test_tui.py` and updated the footer expectation in `tests/test_orchestrator.py`.
handoff_summary.risks: Operator status transitions are intentionally constrained (`ready` from open/blocked/handed_off, `blocked` from open/ready/in_progress/handed_off, `done` from ready/in_progress/handed_off). If downstream expectations differ, the CLI helper and TUI flow will need to be adjusted together.
handoff_summary.next_action: Hand off for review of the new operator-action semantics and keybinding behavior.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` now couples footer/status-panel text and keybinding behavior to the new operator flows; parallel edits in the same areas may conflict with the added runtime state fields and help text. `tests/test_tui.py` and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` now assert the new footer/status behavior and will need coordinated updates if UI wording changes.

conflict_risks: `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` now couples footer/status-panel text and keybinding behavior to the new operator flows; parallel edits in the same areas may conflict with the added runtime state fields and help text. `tests/test_tui.py` and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` now assert the new footer/status behavior and will need coordinated updates if UI wording changes.

execution_history.summary: Implemented TUI operator action handlers for one-shot scheduler runs, continuous run mode, blocked-bead retry, merge confirmation execution, and confirmed single-bead status updates with non-fatal status-panel reporting and scope-aware scheduler invocation.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
